FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class InfoBadge

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI InfoBadge: a small accent (or severity) pill that surfaces a notification count, an icon, or a bare dot. Three display kinds, resolved exactly as WinUI's InfoBadge::OnDisplayKindPropertiesChanged does:

  • Value (Value >= 0): an 11px count, clamped to a 16-tall pill (single digits stay circular because WinUI's MeasureOverride squares a badge narrower than it is tall).
  • Icon (Value < 0, glyph supplied): a Segoe Fluent glyph in the same 16-tall pill, scaled by the WinUI IconPresenter Viewbox to the content-box height (8 for the padded Attention/Informational FontIcon styles and the SymbolIcon severities, 14 for an unpadded FontIcon — see InfoBadgeTemplateSettings).
  • Dot (neither): a bare 4x4 disc.

Background = AccentFillColorDefault (Default), or a SystemFillColor* per InfoBadgeSeverity; foreground = TextOnAccentFillColorPrimary. The corner radius is the computed pill (height/2) from InfoBadgeTemplateSettings.

public static class InfoBadge

GlyphSuccess

Field Value

string

PartRoot

The badge pill itself (WinUI RootGrid — the control's only named element). Owned: Children (the value text / icon glyph — the display-kind content; the Dot has none). The per-kind geometry and severity fill are stock per-render styling a modifier may override.

public const string PartRoot = "Root"

Field Value

string

public static BoxEl Count(int value, InfoBadgeSeverity severity, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

value int
severity InfoBadgeSeverity
parts TemplateParts

Returns

BoxEl

Count(int, ColorF?, TemplateParts?)

The Value badge: a notification count. WinUI clamps to a 16-tall pill; single digits stay circular via the MinWidth = Height square. Pass severity to recolor.

public static BoxEl Count(int value, ColorF? color = null, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

value int
color ColorF?
parts TemplateParts

Returns

Dot(InfoBadgeSeverity, TemplateParts?)

Severity dot — same geometry as Dot(ColorF?, TemplateParts?) with the severity background.

public static BoxEl Dot(InfoBadgeSeverity severity, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

severity InfoBadgeSeverity
parts TemplateParts

Returns

BoxEl

Dot(ColorF?, TemplateParts?)

The bare 4x4 dot. WinUI Dot state (no Value, no Icon): 4x4, corner = 2 (pill at that height).

public static BoxEl Dot(ColorF? color = null, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

color ColorF?
parts TemplateParts

Returns

BoxEl

Icon(InfoBadgeSeverity, TemplateParts?)

Severity icon using WinUI's default per-severity IconSource (Attention 0xEA38, Informational 0xF13F — FontIconSource; Success Accept, Caution Important, Critical Cancel — SymbolIconSource), scaled by the per-state IconPresenter Viewbox target (8 in every severity style; see InfoBadgeTemplateSettings).

public static BoxEl Icon(InfoBadgeSeverity severity, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

severity InfoBadgeSeverity
parts TemplateParts

Returns

BoxEl

Icon(string, InfoBadgeSeverity, TemplateParts?) 

public static BoxEl Icon(string glyph, InfoBadgeSeverity severity, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

glyph string
severity InfoBadgeSeverity
parts TemplateParts

Returns

BoxEl

Icon(string, ColorF?, TemplateParts?)

The Icon badge: a Segoe Fluent glyph in the 16-tall pill. A custom glyph is WinUI's FontIconSource → the FontIcon state (margin 4,0,4,2, InfoBadge_themeresources.xaml:14/:71) — the IconPresenter Viewbox scales the glyph to the content-box height (14 = 16 − 2 for the ad-hoc, no-severity overloads; the Attention/Informational severity styles pad the root 0,4,0,2 → 8).

public static BoxEl Icon(string glyph, ColorF? color = null, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

glyph string
color ColorF?
parts TemplateParts

Returns

BoxEl
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
